2026-04-22.content:
Application Design Center [supports](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/supported-resources) the following components in [General Availability](https://docs.cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages):

* CA Service
* Private Service Connect Endpoint
* Private Service Connect Producer

Application Design Center [supports](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/supported-resources) the following components in [Preview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages):

* Authorization Extension
* Authorization Policy
* Authorization Policy Extension
* Compute Address
* Firebase AI Logic Prompt Template
* Firebase Multi-Platform App
* Routes
* Agent Registry Agent
* Agent Registry Binding
* Agent Registry Endpoint
* Agent Registry MCP Server
* Firestore Security Rules
* IAM Connector
* Model Armor Floor Setting
* Model Armor Template
* VPC Network
* Cloud Workflows
* Firebase AI Logic
* Firebase App Check
* Firebase Authentication
* Compute Firewall
* Cloud KMS
* Internal Load Balancer
* Agent Registry Service
* Artifact Registry
* Cloud Run functions
* Cloud Tasks
* Managed Airflow
* Cloud DNS Managed Zone
* Cloud DNS Response Policy
* Document AI
* Cloud NAT
* Cloud Router
* Cloud Router Interface
* Secure Web Proxy
* Compute Instance
* Cloud Scheduler
* Agent Platform Runtime

summary.title: Application Design Center components now available
summary.summary: The Application Design Center has reached General Availability for several key components, including CA Service, Private Service Connect Endpoint, and Private Service Connect Producer. Additionally, a range of new features are now in Preview, offering expanded capabilities for application development and management.

2026-04-22.content: If your application deployment fails, you can troubleshoot and fix errors [(Preview)](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages). For more information, see [Troubleshoot and fix deployment issues](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/deploy-applications#troubleshoot).

summary.title: Fix Application Deployment Errors
summary.summary: Troubleshoot and resolve issues when your application deployment fails. This new feature helps you identify and fix errors efficiently. For detailed guidance, refer to the documentation on troubleshooting deployment problems.

2026-04-22.content: Create a composite template [(Preview)](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages) using multiple application templates and components. For more information, see [Design composite templates](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/design-composite-templates).

summary.title: Create Composite Templates
summary.summary: You can now create composite templates using multiple application templates and components. This feature allows for more complex and reusable template designs.

2026-04-22.content:
You can store templates and applications in the following regions:

* Tokyo, Japan (asia-northeast1)
* Seoul, South Korea (asia-northeast3)
* Taiwan (asia-east1)
* Hong Kong (asia-east2)
* Delhi, India (asia-south2)
* Singapore (asia-southeast1)
* Jakarta, Indonesia (asia-southeast2)
* Melbourne, Australia (australia-southeast2)
* Hamina, Finland (europe-north1)
* Stockholm, Sweden (europe-north2)
* Warsaw, Poland (europe-central2)
* St. Ghislain, Belgium (europe-west1)
* London, England (europe-west2)
* Frankfurt, Germany (europe-west3)
* Milan, Italy (europe-west8)
* Paris, France (europe-west9)
* Turin, Italy (europe-west12)
* Eemshaven, Netherlands (europe-west4)
* Zurich, Switzerland (europe-west6)
* Madrid, Spain (europe-southwest1)
* Columbus, Ohio (us-east5)
* Ashburn, Virginia (us-east4)
* The Dalles, Oregon (us-west1)
* Los Angeles, California (us-west2)
* Salt Lake City, Utah (us-west3)
* Las Vegas, Nevada (us-west4)
* Council Bluffs, Iowa (us-central1)
* Dallas, Texas (us-south1)
* Montréal, Canada (northamerica-northeast1)
* Toronto, Canada (northamerica-northeast2)
* Queretaro, Mexico (northamerica-south1)
* São Paulo, Brazil (southamerica-east1)
* Santiago, Chile (southamerica-west1)
* Johannesburg, South Africa (africa-south1)
* Doha, Qatar (me-central1)
* Tel Aviv, Israel (me-west1)
* Global

For more information, see the following:

* A list of available [App Design Center locations](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/locations).
* To select and manage a region, see [Select a region](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/manage-spaces#select-region).
* To share a catalog between spaces, see [share a catalog](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/manage-catalogs#share-a-catalog-with-a-space).

summary.title: New regional options available
summary.summary: You can now store templates and applications in a wider range of global regions. This expansion offers greater flexibility and proximity to your users. Explore the newly added locations to optimize your deployments.

2026-04-22.content:
Use the following Google-provided application templates:

* [Single region GKE cluster and workload](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/single-region-gke) [(Preview)](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages)
* [Enterprise-grade production GKE cluster and workload](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/enterprise-grade-production-gke) [(Preview)](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages)
* [AI Pre-trained Inference GKE cluster and workload](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/ai-pretrained-inference-gke-cluster-workload) [(Preview)](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages)

summary.title: New GKE Application Templates Available
summary.summary: Explore our new application templates for GKE, designed to simplify the deployment of single-region, enterprise-grade, and AI-powered workloads. These templates offer a streamlined path to setting up robust and efficient applications on Google Kubernetes Engine.

2025-12-05.content: You can import Terraform modules to create components [(Preview)](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages). For more information, see [Import components](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/import-components).

summary.title: Import Terraform modules
summary.summary: You can now import Terraform modules to create components. This feature is currently in Preview.

2025-12-05.content: You can set up Application Design Center using a single project boundary. For more information, see [Set up Application Design Center](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/setup).

summary.title: Application Design Center Setup
summary.summary: Application Design Center can now be set up within a single project boundary.

2025-12-05.content:
Application Design Center [supports](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/supported-resources) the following components:

* Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) Node Pool
* GKE Standard Cluster
* GKE Autopilot Cluster
* Firestore

summary.title: Application Design Center
summary.summary: Check out the latest features of Application Design Center!

2025-12-05.content: You can export Terraform to your code repository. For more information, see [Export Terraform code](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/download-and-deploy#export_terraform_code).

summary.title: Terraform Export
summary.summary: Export your Terraform code to your repository.

2025-12-05.content:
Application Design Center is generally available [(GA)](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages), including the following:

* [Application Design Center Google Cloud CLI](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/design-center).
* [Design Center API](https://docs.cloud.google.com/application-design-center/docs/reference/rest).

summary.title: Application Design Center is Generally Available
summary.summary: Application Design Center is now generally available. This includes the Application Design Center Google Cloud CLI and the Design Center API.
